metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify AC-6: either specify that this story CREATES ADR-0042 (and describe its required structure) or correct the reference to an existing document (e.g., the new `Autosubmit` label section within `infra/gerrit/project.config`'s header comment, or a new `docs/adr/0042-autosubmit-label-security-invariants.md`) — the current target document does not exist in the repo.
findings.finding: The plan's AC-6 directs the implementer to record the security-invariants entry inside 'the ADR-0042 Security invariants section', treating ADR-0042 as an already-existing document to amend. No such document exists anywhere in the repo (`docs/adr/` goes 0040 → 0041 → 0043 → 0044, with no 0042). The eval spec even explicitly notes 'no ticket it depends_on creates ADR-0042 — it is authored by a DOWNSTREAM story.' An AC that amends a non-existent document is an unbacked premise: either this story must CREATE ADR-0042 (which is undescribed work) or the AC must name the correct document where the invariant will be recorded.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria, AC-6 (Security-invariants artifact)
findings.suggested_fix: Replace 'the ADR-0042 Security invariants section records ...' with 'Create `docs/adr/0042-autosubmit-label-security-invariants.md` and record the non-gating label + bot re-verifies invariant there, using the standard ADR template (Status, Context, Decision, Consequences). Add `grep` evidence as appendix.' This makes the deliverable explicit.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an explicit `label-Autosubmit = -1..+1 group Administrators` line to the ACL specification (alongside the Contributors line) to match the established pattern in `project.config` and preserve the operator override path.
findings.finding: The plan's prose grants the `label-Autosubmit` ACL to 'Contributors (requesters) + Administrators' but the only canonical ACL line specified is `label-Autosubmit = -1..+1 group Contributors`. Every label in the existing `project.config` that has a vote grant also has a matching Administrators line (e.g., `label-LLM-Review = -1..+1 group Service Users` + `label-LLM-Review = -1..+1 group Administrators` at lines 46-47). Without an explicit `label-Autosubmit = -1..+1 group Administrators` line, administrators cannot manually set or clear the Autosubmit label—breaking the operational fallback (e.g., manually revoking a stale Autosubmit+1 if the bot fails to clear it).
findings.location: ## What — ACL line: `[access "refs/heads/*"] label-Autosubmit = -1..+1 group Contributors`; AC#2
findings.suggested_fix:
In the `[access "refs/heads/*"]` block, specify two lines:
```
label-Autosubmit = -1..+1 group Contributors
label-Autosubmit = -1..+1 group Administrators
```
Update AC#2 to list both lines explicitly.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify whether `docs/adr/0042-<slug>.md` is created by THIS ticket (add to Scope/What) or already exists under a different number — the file is absent from the repo and must be either an explicit new artifact or the AC references must be corrected to the right existing ADR.
findings.finding: ADR-0042 does not exist in the repository. AC#4 references 'ADR-0042's security-invariants access-diff' and AC#6 instructs recording an invariant in 'the ADR-0042 Security invariants section,' but `docs/adr/0042-*.md` is absent — the ADR numbering jumps from 0041 to 0043. The plan treats this as an existing document to update, but it is a hallucinated edit target.
findings.location: AC#4 ('ADR-0042's security-invariants access-diff has a committed baseline') and AC#6 ('the ADR-0042 Security invariants section records the non-gating label + bot re-verifies invariant')
findings.suggested_fix: If this ticket authors ADR-0042, add `docs/adr/0042-autosubmit-label-acl.md` (or similar) to the Scope and What sections as a new artifact. If the security-invariants section lives in an existing ADR, replace 'ADR-0042' with its actual path in AC#4 and AC#6.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] State explicitly in the plan whether the `label-Autosubmit` ACL needs a separate `group Administrators` line or whether Administrators are already covered by ALL_PROJECTS inheritance, and add that line to the verbatim ACL block if required.
findings.finding: The plan's prose says the vote ACL is granted to 'Contributors (requesters) + Administrators' but the only verbatim ACL line shown is `label-Autosubmit = -1..+1 group Contributors`. It is unspecified whether the executor must also add a separate `label-Autosubmit = -1..+1 group Administrators` line, or whether Administrators are covered by existing ALL_PROJECTS inheritance. This is a defaultable gap that could cause a wrong diff in AC5's post-cutover access comparison.
findings.location: What section: 'ACL line: [access "refs/heads/*"] label-Autosubmit = -1..+1 group Contributors' and AC2
findings.suggested_fix: Add a second ACL line to the verbatim block: `label-Autosubmit = -1..+1 group Administrators` (if an explicit grant is needed), or add a parenthetical: '(Administrators covered by ALL_PROJECTS `label-Autosubmit` = -1..+1 group Administrators grant; no new line needed)' to remove ambiguity.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Expand the Scope (or What) section to explicitly name updating the ADR-0042 Security invariants section as a deliverable in this story, reconciling it with AC#6 and removing the contradiction with 'Config + ACL + cutover ONLY'.
findings.finding: AC#6 requires updating the ADR-0042 Security invariants section (recording the non-gating invariant with concrete checks), but the Scope section declares 'Config + ACL + cutover ONLY' — contradicting AC#6's demand for an ADR documentation deliverable.
findings.location: Scope section vs. AC#6
findings.suggested_fix: Append to the Scope section: 'Also in scope: adding the non-gating label security invariant (with concrete grep checks) to the ADR-0042 Security invariants section.' and adjust the one-liner to read 'Config + ACL + cutover + ADR-0042 security-invariants record ONLY.'

findings.finding: AC #5 mandates a 'Security invariants artifact' recorded in 'ADR-0042', but neither the 'What' section nor 'Scope' describes authoring or updating ADR-0042 as a deliverable. This is an orphan criterion with no corresponding described work.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria #5 vs. 'What' and 'Scope' sections
findings.suggested_fix: In the 'What' section, add a bullet: 'Author the ADR-0042 Security invariants section documenting the non-gating Autosubmit label invariant (NoBlock, no submit-requirement reference) and the bot's is:submittable re-verify gate.' In 'Scope', amend to: 'Config + ACL + cutover + ADR-0042 security invariants section.'

findings.finding: Acceptance-criteria / definition-of-done quality issues.
findings.suggested_fix: Split compound criteria, replace subjective terms with observable outcomes, and state how each is verified.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add `infra/gerrit/setup-project.sh` to the plan's edit targets (or explicitly note it only needs a commit-message update) so the audit trail in refs/meta/config history correctly reflects the Autosubmit label cutover.
findings.finding: `setup-project.sh` (the operator tool that pushes `project.config` to `refs/meta/config`) contains a hardcoded commit message (line 148) that will become stale once the `Autosubmit` label is added, and — more importantly — is the ONLY push path for refs/meta/config but is not named as a file to update in the plan's Scope, What, or ACs. If the plan's cutover is performed via `setup-project.sh`, the script is a de-facto edit target that must at minimum have its commit message updated.
findings.location: Scope section ('Config + ACL + cutover ONLY'); infra/gerrit/setup-project.sh line 148
findings.suggested_fix: Update the commit message string in setup-project.sh line 148 to include '+ Autosubmit label + vote ACL' when applying this story's changes.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a concrete structured diff command to AC5 (e.g., `jq --sort-keys . pre.json > pre_sorted.json && jq --sort-keys . post.json > post_sorted.json && diff pre_sorted.json post_sorted.json`) so the 'no other ACL drift' assertion is deterministic and anchored.
findings.finding: AC5's post-cutover verification assertion — 'a post-cutover GET /a/projects/rebar/access diffs cleanly against infra/gerrit/access-snapshot-pre-autolander.json showing ONLY the added label-Autosubmit vote grant' — names no concrete diff/comparison command, leaving the verification method implicit and potentially error-prone (verify-command defect type 6: prose-vs-structured assertion target).
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria, AC5
findings.suggested_fix: Append to AC5: 'Proved via: `curl -u $GERRIT_CREDS $BASE/a/projects/rebar/access | jq --sort-keys . > /tmp/post.json && diff <(jq --sort-keys . infra/gerrit/access-snapshot-pre-autolander.json) /tmp/post.json` — the only diff line must be the label-Autosubmit grant addition.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an explicit rollback procedure to the plan (e.g., 'if post-cutover diff shows unexpected ACL drift, revert by pushing the pre-cutover `project.config` captured in `access-snapshot-pre-autolander.json` back to `refs/meta/config`'), satisfying T4's requirement for a documented back-out path.
findings.finding: The plan describes a live `refs/meta/config` push (cutover) as an explicit, justified step, but provides no rollback or back-out procedure. The pre-cutover snapshot (AC#4) supplies the data needed to revert, but no rollback step is articulated, leaving the irreversible operation without a documented recovery path.
findings.location: AC#4, AC#5; What section ('live cutover applied to refs/meta/config')
findings.suggested_fix: Add a 'Rollback' subsection under What or as an additional AC: 'If the post-cutover diff (AC#5) shows any ACL change beyond the expected label-Autosubmit grant, revert by re-pushing the project.config state corresponding to access-snapshot-pre-autolander.json to refs/meta/config and re-verifying via GET /a/projects/rebar/access.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an AC line (or a CLAUDE.md / ADR-0042 subsection) that commits the canonical label name (`Autosubmit`) and group name (`Contributors`) as the stable bot/agent interface contract, so downstream stories and the security-invariants diff have a single committed reference point.
findings.finding: The plan declares `Autosubmit` (label name) and `Contributors` (group name) as the canonical cross-story contract that 'the epic and downstream stories reference,' but this contract lives only in plan prose — there is no committed artifact (CLAUDE.md entry, ADR section, or dedicated contract file) that durably records these names as the stable interface for consumers such as S4's `land` command and the auto-lander bot. If either name is revised after this story ships, downstream stories have no single committed reference to diff against.
findings.location: ## What — 'This canonical account/group naming is what the epic and downstream stories reference.'
findings.suggested_fix: Add to the ADR-0042 Security invariants section (or a new `## Interface contract` subsection of the same doc) a table: `label: Autosubmit`, `vote-group: Contributors`, `range: -1..+1`, `function: NoBlock` — and reference it explicitly in an AC line: 'ADR-0042 records the canonical label/group names consumed by S4 land and the auto-lander bot.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a remediation step to AC 5: define what the operator must do if the post-cutover ACL diff shows entries beyond the expected `label-Autosubmit` grant (e.g., revert the `refs/meta/config` push, investigate and re-baseline, then re-apply).
findings.finding: The plan specifies that the post-cutover ACL diff must show 'ONLY the added label-Autosubmit vote grant (no other ACL drift)' but provides no remediation or rollback procedure if unexpected drift IS detected. In a security-sensitive context (ADR-0042 security invariants), discovering drift without a defined response path leaves the operator without guidance and could mean a broken security baseline is left in place.
findings.location: AC item 5 — '…diffs cleanly against infra/gerrit/access-snapshot-pre-autolander.json showing ONLY the added label-Autosubmit vote grant (no other ACL drift)'
findings.suggested_fix: Append to AC 5: 'If the diff shows any entry beyond the `label-Autosubmit` grant, the operator MUST revert the `refs/meta/config` push immediately, investigate the source of drift, update `access-snapshot-pre-autolander.json` to reflect the current state, and re-apply the label change after the drift is resolved and documented.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a RETIRE path note to the plan (even a forward-reference is sufficient): specify how the `Autosubmit` label and its `label-Autosubmit` ACL grant will be removed from `project.config` and `refs/meta/config` if the auto-lander feature is decommissioned, and how open changes bearing the vote will be handled.
findings.finding: The plan defines no RETIRE/cleanup path for the `Autosubmit` label config itself. If the feature is deprecated, renamed, or superseded, there is no specified procedure for removing the label definition from `project.config`, handling open changes that still carry the vote, or revoking the Contributors ACL grant. Per the rubric, shared state with no retirement path is a MAJOR gap.
findings.location: ## Scope — 'Config + ACL + cutover ONLY'; AC items 1–2 define CREATE but no RETIRE.
findings.suggested_fix: Add a 'Retirement' subsection: 'To retire this label: (1) remove the `[label "Autosubmit"]` stanza and `label-Autosubmit` ACL line from `project.config`; (2) push to `refs/meta/config`; (3) update the access snapshot baseline; (4) any open changes bearing an `Autosubmit` vote will have that vote silently invalidated by Gerrit on label removal — no further action required.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Verify and document what 'S4's `land`' refers to: if it is a future story, cite it by ticket ID and note it does not yet exist; if it is an existing capability, name the actual module/command path so a reviewer can confirm the ACL satisfies it.
findings.finding: The plan's What section states the vote ACL is granted to 'Contributors (requesters) so an agent can set the label under its OWN Gerrit identity (S4's `land`)'. This treats 'S4's `land`' as a named, already-existing CLI command or agent action. A thorough search of the repo finds no `land` subcommand, no S4 story that ships a `land` command, and no module named `land` — S4 across this codebase refers to the gerrit CI service-account story (epic 1fa8) and the review-bot voter story (d251/S4b), neither of which defines a `land` verb. The assertion that this label's purpose is to serve 'S4's `land`' rests on a future artifact that does not yet exist.
findings.location: What section: 'so an agent can set the label under its OWN Gerrit identity (S4's `land`)'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a brief prior-art note in the plan (or a linked ADR) acknowledging the AOSP/Chromium `Autosubmit`/`CQ+1` opt-in label lineage and confirming the design intentionally follows (or intentionally departs from) that established pattern, satisfying ADR-0040's R6 principle.
findings.finding: The plan does not acknowledge the well-established AOSP/Chromium `Autosubmit` (or `Auto-Submit`) project-label pattern, which serves exactly the same opt-in 'please queue this for landing' intent. Since the plan already invokes ADR-0040's R6 principle ('emulate a proven, actively-maintained OSS workflow rather than invent one'), failing to cite this lineage leaves the reader unable to assess whether the design diverges intentionally from the Android/Chromium precedent or simply rediscovers it.
findings.location: ## Why / ## What
findings.suggested_fix: In the ## Why or ## What section, add: 'This follows the AOSP/Chromium opt-in label pattern (Android Gerrit `Autosubmit +1`, Chromium `Commit-Queue +1`) where Contributors flag a change for bot-driven landing. Departure: we name the label `Autosubmit` (not `Commit-Queue`) and grant the vote to `Contributors` rather than a separate `bot-trusted` tier, consistent with our existing landing-authorization group design.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Either (a) add an explicit story dependency / blocking pre-condition linking this story's ACL cutover to the downstream bot story's re-verify-at-submit implementation, or (b) add an AC line requiring that the label ACL is pushed to `refs/meta/config` only AFTER the bot story's re-verify contract is specified and testable, so the security invariant is verifiable before the label is live.
findings.finding: The plan's core security argument — 'a requester-votable ACL is not a security vector' — rests on an unverified capability assertion about a not-yet-built system: 'the bot still requires `is:submittable` (both real votes MAX) and re-verifies at submit.' The bot that reads the `Autosubmit` label is explicitly out of scope ('The service that reads the label is a separate story'). There is no specified mechanism to ensure the downstream story actually implements the re-verify before the label is live and the ACL is in effect.
findings.location: ## What; AC (last bullet — security-invariants artifact)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Confirm the exact Gerrit 3.14.1 REST path for single-label lookup (e.g., probe `GET /a/projects/rebar/labels/Autosubmit` against the live server before shipping, or replace with `GET /a/projects/rebar/labels` + grep for `Autosubmit` in the response, which is confirmed to work for the list-labels surface).
findings.finding: AC#5 requires verifying the label via `GET /a/projects/rebar/labels/Autosubmit` (singular label by name), but this specific REST endpoint surface (`/projects/{project}/labels/{label-name}`) does not appear in any live repo script, runbook, or prior evidence file — it is Unverified. The Gerrit Projects REST API does expose a list-labels endpoint, but the exact path form and its availability on Gerrit 3.14.1 (the deployed version) is not confirmed.
findings.location: AC#5: 'verified via `GET /a/projects/rebar/labels/Autosubmit`'
findings.suggested_fix: Replace the AC#5 verification step with `GET /a/projects/rebar/labels` (the list-labels endpoint, which is a standard Gerrit Projects REST sub-resource) and assert that `Autosubmit` appears in the JSON response — this avoids the single-label-by-name path ambiguity.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the credential (account + credential store) required to call `GET /a/projects/rebar/access` and add a probe/sample call to an existing runbook or the setup-project.sh DRY_RUN path to confirm the endpoint is accessible and returns the expected JSON shape before the cutover change is shipped.
findings.finding: AC#4 and AC#5 both require calling `GET /a/projects/rebar/access` (to capture a pre-cutover baseline and post-cutover diff), but no live repo script, runbook, or evidence document actually calls this endpoint — it is Partially-verified. The auth credentials (which account, which credential store) to be used for these operator calls are not specified in the plan.
findings.location: AC#4: 'save the current `GET /a/projects/rebar/access` response'; AC#5: 'a post-cutover `GET /a/projects/rebar/access` diffs cleanly'

findings.finding: The REST proving command `GET /projects/rebar/labels/Autosubmit` in AC4 has no structured assertion on the response fields, so it can pass trivially (HTTP 200 with wrong content) without proving `function = NoBlock` or empty `copyCondition`.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria, AC4: 'verified via `GET /projects/rebar/labels/Autosubmit`'
findings.suggested_fix: Add to AC4: `curl -gsS 'https://<host>/projects/rebar/labels/Autosubmit' | jq -e '.function == "NoBlock" and (.copy_condition // "") == "" and (.values | has("+1")) and (.values | has("-1"))'` must exit 0.

findings.finding: The cutover verification step in AC4 cites 'GET /projects/rebar/labels/Autosubmit' (no /a/ prefix), but every authenticated Gerrit REST call in this codebase uses the /a/ digest-auth prefix (e.g. /a/accounts/self in smoke-check.sh, /a/projects/rebar/config in the rollback runbook). The /a/projects/{project}/labels/{label} sub-resource is a new endpoint surface that has never been exercised in the repo; the plan provides no credential/curl invocation for it and does not state whether it requires admin auth. If the wrong path is used the call silently 404s, leaving the cutover unverified.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria #4: 'verified via GET /projects/rebar/labels/Autosubmit'
findings.suggested_fix: Change AC4 to: `curl -sS -u "${GERRIT_SSH_USER}:${GERRIT_ADMIN_HTTP_TOKEN}" "https://${GERRIT_HOST}/a/projects/rebar/labels/Autosubmit"` and assert the response contains `"function": "NO_BLOCK"` — matching the pattern in smoke-check.sh and the rollback runbook.

findings.finding: The plan's key security argument ('the bot still requires `is:submittable` (both real votes MAX) and re-verifies at submit') is a decision rule referenced in both the What section and AC5's security-invariants artifact, but the rule is never specified: what exactly the bot checks, in what order, on which SHA, and what it does if the check fails is not defined here or in any discoverable existing ADR/file. The Scope explicitly defers this to a separate story with no placeholder protocol.
findings.location: What section: 'the bot still requires `is:submittable` (both real votes MAX) and re-verifies at submit, so a requester-votable ACL is not a security vector'; AC5: 'the bot only submits when `is:submittable` holds on the tested SHA'

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
